Finkbine Golf Course (Iowa City, Iowa - the home course for the University of Iowa) has combo tees not currently supported in 18Birdies. The tees currently supported are (longest to shortest) black, gold, white, and gray. In addition, the scorecard lists three sets of combo tees: black/gold, gold/white, and white/gray that are not currently available in 18birdies. All three sets of combo tees have official rating/slope shown on the scorecard. (There are also rating/slope for women from gold, white, and gray tees.)

I am attaching a jpg of the scorecard downloaded from their website (https://www.finkbine.com/golf/golf/course/scorecards).

I appreciate how quickly you get new tees added to the app. The last time I submitted one (Pleasant Valley (Iowa City, Iowa) white/red combo tees), you had the tees added to the app in a day or two. Excellent support is appreciated! Thank you!

-- Doug Hiratzka

Hi 18Birdies Support Team,

Thank you for getting back to me. I want to clarify my original report, as I think it has been misunderstood - this is not a request to update the course scorecard or add missing tees. The course data (hole distances, par, etc.) appears correct. The issue is a GPS map alignment problem: the map overlay used to calculate GPS-based yardages is offset from the actual physical location of the course features.

---

COURSE DETAILS

Course: Bowen Island Golf Course

Location: Bowen Island, British Columbia, Canada

Club ID (from app): 75afa620-86ac-11e4-8c28-020000005b00

---

PROBLEM DESCRIPTION

When using the 18 Birdies GPS feature on the course, the yardages shown are consistently 5-15 yards different to the actual distance to the target. This issue affects every hole on the course and is experienced by multiple golfers, ruling out individual phone GPS error as the cause.

The symptom is consistent with the app's internal map coordinates being shifted slightly from the true GPS position of the course - meaning the app "thinks" we are standing somewhere we are not, or that the green/target markers are placed slightly away from their true positions.

---

CONTROLLED TEST - HOLE 8, WHITE TEE BOX

I conducted a side-by-side comparison on the course to demonstrate the discrepancy. All measurements were taken from the same physical location: the white tee box on Hole 8.

Compass bearing to green: approximately 50° (north-northeast)

18 Birdies GPS readings (see attached Screenshot 1):

- To middle of green (pin): 182 yards

- To back of green: 195 yards

- To front of green: 168 yards

Garmin Golf app GPS readings (see attached Screenshot 2):

- To pin: 175 yards

- To back of green: 188 yards

- To front of green: 168 yards

Feedback: Hello 18Birdies Support Team,

I’m reaching out to report a consistent hole-mapping issue on 18Birdies for Bass River Golf Course.

After cross-referencing the in-app hole sequence with the official course scorecard, it appears that the entire back 9 is incorrectly rotated. This is not a yardage error, but a mislabeling of hole numbers.

Here is the exact discrepancy:

18Birdies currently labels the back 9 holes as follows:
• Hole 10 → actually Hole 16
• Hole 11 → actually Hole 17
• Hole 12 → actually Hole 18
• Hole 13 → actually Hole 10
• Hole 14 → actually Hole 11
• Hole 15 → actually Hole 12
• Hole 16 → actually Hole 13
• Hole 17 → actually Hole 14
• Hole 18 → actually Hole 15

This indicates a circular rotation error where the back 9 is shifted forward by three holes and wrapped incorrectly.

Impact of the issue:
• GPS yardages are applied to the wrong holes, affecting club selection
• Score tracking and hole-by-hole stats (GIR, FIR, putts) become inaccurate
• Course flow does not match the actual routing, which can confuse users during play

Suggested fix:
• Re-anchor Hole 10 to the correct starting hole (actual Hole 10)
• Shift the back 9 hole sequence backward by three positions
• Validate the corrected order against the official scorecard to ensure proper sequencing (10 → 11 → 12 → … → 18)

This appears to be a data indexing issue rather than incorrect course data, so a simple reordering of the hole sequence should resolve it.

Thank you for your time and for maintaining a great app.

Nate West (nowest92@gmail.com)
